archetype: Archetype 1 (Organic & Earthy) mixed with Archetype 4 (Swiss Brutalist)
concept: The Botanical Canvas. A fusion of highly organic botanical elements (flowers, serif typography, natural palettes) and precise technical UI tools (Figma bounding boxes, monospace annotations, anchor points, grid lines).

typography.instructions: Use Cormorant Garamond for all primary headings, frequently italicizing specific words to mimic organic, vine-like curves. Use JetBrains Mono for 'UI' elements like tooltips, labels, and technical annotations. Use Outfit for readable paragraphs.

layout_and_spacing.spacing_philosophy: Generous organic spacing (p-12, gap-16) interrupted by technical, dense grids where needed.
layout_and_spacing.grid_system: Bento 'Tetris' grid combined with CSS background grid patterns (`bg-[radial-gradient(#e5e7eb_1px,transparent_1px)] bg-[size:24px_24px]`).

figma_card.appearance: White background, sharp 0px border-radius, 1px solid border. Default border is #E5E5E5. On hover, border turns dashed and Figma Blue (#1ABCFE).
figma_card.decorations: Add absolute positioned 6px x 6px white squares with blue borders at the corners on hover to mimic Figma anchor/resize nodes.
figma_card.usage: Use for Project cards and About section containers.

annotation_label.appearance: Tiny floating badges attached to sections. JetBrains Mono font, text-xs, bg-white, 1px solid #E5E5E5, or filled with Figma Blue/Red with white text.
annotation_label.usage: Place these above or overlapping images/sections, reading like layer names (e.g., '🌱 hero-frame', 'W: 1200 H: 800', 'Auto Layout').

buttons.primary: Sharp corners, bg-[#1A1A1A] text-white hover:bg-[#1ABCFE] hover:text-white transition-colors duration-300 font-mono text-sm px-6 py-3 uppercase tracking-wider
buttons.secondary: Sharp corners, bg-transparent text-[#1A1A1A] border border-[#1A1A1A] hover:bg-[#E07A5F] hover:text-white hover:border-[#E07A5F] transition-colors duration-300 font-mono text-sm px-6 py-3 uppercase tracking-wider

motion_and_interactions.hover_effects: Hovering an image or card should trigger 'Figma selection' styles (blue outlines, measuring lines appearing, node squares).
motion_and_interactions.draggable_elements: Use framer-motion `drag` on certain scattered floating elements in the Hero and Playground.
motion_and_interactions.cursor: Implement a custom framer-motion cursor that looks like the Figma 'Move' tool (SVG pointer), perhaps with an attached nametag reading 'Designer'.

sections_instructions.Hero: Massive 'Cormorant Garamond' heading: 'Cultivating Digital Experiences'. Behind/around the text, scatter draggable `framer-motion` 'frames' containing botanical images or solid green/terracotta colors. Include a background dot grid. Add 'Figma' cursor elements.
sections_instructions.About: Split layout. Left: A 'Properties Panel' resembling Figma's right sidebar (Design, Prototype, Inspect tabs) but displaying the designer's details, tools, and stats in monospace. Right: A beautifully typeset organic narrative in serif.
sections_instructions.Projects: Grid of Figma-style component cards. When hovering over a card, reveal red 'measuring guides' showing the dimensions in pixels, and slightly zoom the image. Images should blend UI mockups with botanical elements.
sections_instructions.Skills: Designed exactly like a Figma 'Layers' panel. A vertical list with Lucide icons (Layers, Image, Type, Hash) representing different skills (e.g., '# UI Design', 'T Typography', '❖ Prototyping').
sections_instructions.Interactive_Playground: A large canvas area. Include a note saying 'Drag elements around'. Scatter random SVG flowers, sticky notes (yellow rectangles with hand-written font/serif), and UI components. Let the user compose them.
sections_instructions.Contact: Minimalist. A giant mailto link styled as a 'Component' waiting to be dropped. Use data-testid on the mail link.
sections_instructions.Floating_Chatbot: Positioned bottom-right. The trigger button should be a sparkling star/sparkle icon in Figma Purple (#A259FF). When opened, the UI should look exactly like Figma's commenting system (rounded corners, white bg, slight drop shadow, input at bottom, simple chat bubbles). Title it 'Design Critique AI'.

media_assets.icons_library: lucide-react (mandatory for the Figma tool UI icons: MousePointer2, Frame, Layers, Type, Hash, Sparkles)

testing.attributes: Every interactive element MUST include a 'data-testid' attribute (e.g., data-testid='hero-cta-button', data-testid='chatbot-trigger', data-testid='project-card-1').

forbidden_elements: No generic purple gradients (except the strict Figma #A259FF used only for the AI icon)
No Inter, Roboto, or standard sans-serifs for headings. Use Cormorant Garamond.
No centered card grids. Use bento or asymmetrical layouts with 'measuring lines'.
No rounded 'Apple/Vercel' style cards. Cards must be sharp (rounded-none) to mimic artboards.
